questions.question: What is a friend function in C++? Explain its characteristics.
keyPoints: A friend function is a non-member function declared with 'friend' keyword inside a class
It has access to private and protected members of the class
It is not a member of the class and cannot be called using object.operator()
It does not have a 'this' pointer
Friendship is not inherited or transitive

questions.question: Explain the differences between member functions and friend functions.
keyPoints: Member functions belong to the class; friend functions do not
Member functions have 'this' pointer; friend functions do not
Member functions are called using object; friend functions called like normal functions
Member functions can be virtual/const; friend functions cannot
Member functions participate in inheritance; friend functions do not

questions.question: What are the advantages and limitations of using friend functions?
keyPoints: Advantages: Access to private members, useful for operator overloading, allows functions to work with multiple objects
Limitations: Friendship is not transitive or reciprocal, cannot be inherited, does not have 'this' pointer, can break encapsulation if overused

questions.question: Define a friend class and explain its significance with an example.
keyPoints: A friend class is a class whose all member functions become friend functions of another class
All member functions of the friend class can access private/protected members
Useful when two classes need close collaboration
Example: A Stack class might have a FriendArray class to directly access internal array

questions.question: Write a C++ program to demonstrate the use of friend function to add two complex numbers.
keyPoints: Define a Complex class with real and imaginary parts
Declare a friend function add() that takes two Complex objects
Function returns a new Complex object with summed values
Show output displaying the result

questions.question: Explain why friendship is not transitive and not reciprocal with examples.
keyPoints: Not transitive: If A is friend of B, and B is friend of C, A is not automatically friend of C
Not reciprocal: If A declares B as friend, B does not automatically become friend of A
This maintains encapsulation and prevents unintended access

questions.question: Write a C++ program to create a class Box with length, breadth, and height as private members. Use friend functions to calculate the volume of the box and to compare two boxes to find which has larger volume.
keyPoints: Define Box class with three private data members
Create constructor to initialize values
Declare friend functions: volume() and compareVolume()
volume() should take Box object and return volume
compareVolume() should take two Box objects and return reference to larger one
Main function demonstrates both operations
Output shows volume and comparison result
